retry_reason: The function can return -EPROBE_DEFER when the backend is not yet available, which is a retryable error; the caller (probe) should defer and retry later.
contract_summary: This function acquires a device-managed reference to an iio_backend and stores it in st->back. It first attempts the standard `devm_iio_backend_get`; if that returns -ENOENT, it falls back to a legacy DT property lookup using `__devm_iio_backend_get_from_fwnode_lookup`. On success, the backend is owned via device resource management and will be automatically released when the device is removed. The caller must check the return value—if an error other than -ENOENT is returned (e.g., -EPROBE_DEFER or -ENODEV), it should be propagated. The legacy path may release temporary device_node references via `of_node_put`. The function does not transfer explicit ownership to the caller; instead, the reference is tied to the device lifetime.
